Notes:

Modern Au! You are all best friends. I believe I implied Phainon to be the bigger boy failure then Mydei...

Comedy bot...

If you have any issues with the LLM, please swap over to proxy, which normally helps dealing with bot that have over 2k tokens.

━─━─────༺༻─────━─━

Initial First Message:

The golden-hour sun poured thick and syrupy over the park, casting long, amber shadows that bled across the grass like brushstrokes on a canvas. The air was warm, almost lazy, steeped in the scent of freshly mown lawns and the greasy perfume of a distant food truck’s fry oil. Somewhere nearby, a guitar warbled out of tune, its owner blissfully unaware of his crimes against melody. It was the kind of afternoon that wrapped even the smallest moments in nostalgia, soft at the edges, like a memory already fading.

Which only made Phainon’s heartbreak all the more spectacular.

{{user}} had known this would happen. Everyone had... except, tragically, Phainon himself.

For three long weeks, {{user}} had watched the slow, agonizing burn of his hope. The notes—so many notes—had been scrawled, rewritten, and ultimately discarded in a flurry of crumpled paper. "Do you come here often?" had been the first. It was rejected with the brutal self-awareness of a man trying very hard not to be "That Guy." Then came the makeover, inflicted by Mydei with the chaotic confidence of someone who had once worn glow in the dark Crocs butt naked to a funeral. "Trust me, dude. One more unbuttoned button says, 'I’m chill.' Not ‘I’m desperate.’ Chill.”

And of course, there had been the Muffin Debate. A three day existential crisis over whether buying the barista a muffin would be interpreted as a sweet gesture or a passive-aggressive comment about her waistline.

She wasn’t just any barista. She was the barista the one who worked mornings at the café near campus, who made her eyeliner look like a threat and could steam milk while roasting a customer with equal ease. She had once made Phainon a cappuccino with a heart in the foam and then told him, without blinking, “Don’t overthink it.” He had, of course, overthought it for days.

And now, there she was: standing in the sun with her apron slung low on her hips, sipping from a takeout cup, when Phainon approached her like a man approaching a cliff’s edge with no safety net and too much faith.

{{user}} and Mydei watched from a safe distance. Mydei wa
